title: Set up and mark an Assignment
staff_situations: How do I set up and mark an Assignment?
underlying_needs: A clear, consistent way to set work, collect submissions and return feedback in one place
Marking done digitally means no lost paperwork, and learners always have a saved record of their feedback
A way to see who's completed work and how the group is doing on grades, without having to chase every submission individually

accessibility: Digital rubrics — the marking criteria built into Assignments — and inline feedback can both be read using a screen reader — software that reads the screen aloud — or a text-to-speech tool
Learners can look back at the assignment instructions and their feedback whenever they need to, at their own pace
inclusive_practice: Consistent assignment structure means every learner knows what to expect and where to find it
Submitting work digitally gives learners another way to hand it in if writing by hand is difficult for them

framework_alignment: Digital Skills Framework: assignments and feedback
